prompt: Construct a Collegiate Resource & Housing Governance Console supporting offline administration of student housing records, academic resources, employer content compliance, and operational reporting for a US-based education provider. The desktop app is built with PyQt to deliver a keyboard-first, multi-window workflow on Windows 11, with a system tray presence for quick access and background rule execution. Roles include System Administrator (configures master data and security), Housing Coordinator (maintains bed assignments and move history), Academic Admin (manages the resource catalog and publishing), Compliance Reviewer (reviews employer onboarding and violations), and Operations Analyst (runs dashboards and exports). Users navigate via tabbed workspaces plus detachable windows (e.g., "Student Profile," "Resource Catalog," "Compliance Queue"), right-click context menus for common actions (assign bed, publish version, place item on hold), and global shortcuts such as Ctrl+K for universal search, Ctrl+Shift+N to create a new record, and Ctrl+E to export the current result set. A local notification and messaging center provides in-app messages only (no SMS/email/official-account delivery), with template variables like {StudentName}, {Dorm}, and {EffectiveDate}; rules can be trigger-based (e.g., bed reassignment, resource publish/unpublish, violation decision) or scheduled (daily 7:00 AM digest), requiring delivery receipts within the app and retry up to 3 times at 5-minute intervals if a local queue write fails. Search supports structured filters and full-text lookup across students, resources, employers, and compliance cases with fuzzy matching and synonym sets managed by admins; saved searches can be pinned to the sidebar and exported to CSV. All business logic runs locally with data persisted in a SQLite database encrypted at rest using an app-managed key; sensitive fields (SSN fragments if stored, phone numbers, personal email) must be masked by default and revealed only to authorized roles after password re-entry. Student and housing profiles maintain master data (name, student ID, college/class, contact info), housing status, bed assignment, and immutable historical change logs with timestamp and operator ID; bulk import/export supports CSV and Excel files with validation (required columns, date formats MM/DD/YYYY, duplicate student ID handling) and a "dry-run" preview before commit. The unified resource catalog stores a hierarchical tree with custom resource types and metadata templates (field types, regex validation, required flags), versioning, publish/unpublish state, tags, and relationships to subject/grade/course; publication requires a reviewer approval and increments a semantic version (e.g., 2.3.0). Employer onboarding captures verification documents (locally stored file attachments with SHA-256 fingerprints), performs sensitive-word checks using an offline dictionary, logs reports with evidence retention for 7 years, and enforces violation actions (takedown, account suspension for 30/60/180 days, or traffic throttling by hiding content from default searches). Style master data and multi-version BOM/process-routing management supports change requests, two-step approval, automatic cost recalculation in USD with a locked audit trail, and historical traceability for any released version. The app must start in under 5 seconds on a typical office PC, remain stable for 8-hour sessions with explicit resource cleanup for closed windows, provide automatic state recovery after crashes (reopen last workspace and restore unsaved drafts from checkpoints every 60 seconds), and support offline updates via importing a signed update package with a rollback option. Installation is delivered as a Windows .msi, and the UI must be usable at 1920x1080 and above with High DPI scaling.
